office_does: A state representative writes and votes on state laws and budgets, conducts oversight, and represents their legislative district.
race_blurb: The open-seat field includes Donovan Arnold DeLeon, Luc Jasmin III, Natalie Poulson, and Pam Kohlmeier.

candidates.summary: DeLeon is a paratransit operator, former business owner and caregiver, and first-time candidate emphasizing workers, health care, housing, transportation, and LGBTQ rights.
highlights: Supports accessible transportation, worker protections, and LGBTQ civil rights [S2].

experience.basis: The dossier explicitly identifies him as a first-time candidate with no elected record.

social.basis: He explicitly emphasizes LGBTQ civil rights.

candidates.summary: Jasmin is an early-learning business owner and education advocate emphasizing child care, public education, housing, small businesses, and opportunity.
highlights: Emphasizes child-care access and public education [S2].

experience.basis: He has policy and community-organization experience but no documented elected tenure.

candidates.summary: Poulson emphasizes lower living costs, public safety, education, parental involvement, and accountable spending.
highlights: Supports public safety, parental involvement, and accountable spending [S2].

spending.basis: She explicitly emphasizes accountable state spending.

experience.basis: The dossier documents no elected or institutional public-office record.

social.basis: She explicitly emphasizes parental involvement in education.

candidates.summary: Kohlmeier is a physician, attorney, and former Medical Commission policy manager emphasizing health care, public health, evidence-based government, affordability, and reproductive rights.
highlights: Brings health and regulatory-policy experience and supports reproductive rights [S2].

experience.basis: She has public-policy management experience but no documented elected tenure.
